document.summary: The dataset provides information on military expenditure in current USD for the region of Africa Eastern and Southern from 1994 to 2025. It includes annual military expenditure figures, showing a general trend of increase over the years. The most recent data point for 2024 indicates military expenditure of approximately 13.66 billion USD.
key_takeaways: Military expenditure data spans from 1994 to 2025.
The highest recorded expenditure was approximately 16.17 billion USD in 2017.
Expenditure in 2024 is projected at 13.66 billion USD.
There is a general upward trend in military spending over the years.
The data is sourced from the World Bank.
The dataset includes a total of 17,556 entries.
document.why_it_matters: Understanding military expenditure is crucial for analyzing defense budgets, economic priorities, and regional security dynamics in Africa Eastern and Southern. This data can inform policymakers and researchers about trends in military spending and its implications for development and stability.

claims.claim_text: Military expenditure in Africa Eastern and Southern has increased over the years.
claims.evidence_text: The dataset shows a general trend of increase in military expenditure from 1994 to 2024.

claims.claim_text: The military expenditure for 2024 is approximately 13.66 billion USD.
claims.evidence_text: The military expenditure for 2024 is recorded as 13659048840.3166 USD.
